sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.125 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Does every finite group$G$contain a pair of conjugate elements$a,b$such that$(G)= ( a,b )$? This is true for soluble groups. Comment of 2013: it was proved in (A.\,Lucchini, M.\,Morigi, P.\,Shumyatsky, Forum Math., { 24} (2012), 875--887) that every finite group$G$contains a 2-generator subgroup$H$such that$(G)= (H)$. { P.\,Shumyatsky}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
